headline: Sweetgreen Pares 26% Drop as Parasite Tied to Taco Bell Lettuce
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Sweetgreen Inc. shares surged on Friday, halting a four-day skid, after US regulators said a parasite outbreak that’s sickened thousands across several states was traced to shredded iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell restaurants. headline: Parasite concerns cast a shadow over fast-food stocks
summary: Opening Bid host Brian Sozzi tackles the Question of the Day: Should investors rotate away from fast-food stocks amid the parasite outbreak?

headline: The cyclosporiasis outbreak linked to Taco Bell is hitting some restaurant stocks, but don't expect a long-term impact
summary: Taco Bell, which the CDC linked to the cyclosporiasis outbreak, will likely recover soon from the health safety scare, according to analysts.

headline: Sweetgreen bounces back as Taco Bell takes the heat for produce panic
summary: Investors are finally digesting the lettuce panic, and salad chains are breathing a sigh of relief. Sweetgreen Inc. (NYSE: SG) caught a welcome 2.7% tailwind on Friday, beginning to claw back a brutal 23% slide over the past five days. The culprit for the recent freefall? Guilt by association. Wall Street dumped produce-heavy restaurant stocks as the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) investigated a widespread Cyclospora parasite outbreak. headline: Investigators eye Taylor Farms lettuce at Taco Bell as potential source of cyclosporiasis 'explosive' diarrhea outbreak
summary: The parasite has sickened thousands and put more than 4,300 people in one corner of Michigan at the center of the outbreak.
